title: Listening - Part 4: The Venture of Moving Abroad for Work
description: You will hear five short extracts in which experiences of people who moved abroad for work and how it changed their lives. You must complete two tasks while listening.

content.audio_script: Speaker 1: When I first considered the idea of moving to Germany for work, it was [21]the potential for career advancement[/21] that drew me in. The company I was with had significant projects there. Little did I know how fulfilling the experience would be. Living in a different culture was eye-opening. The most rewarding part, though, was [26]the personal growth I experienced[/26]. My adaptability improved, and I met people from diverse backgrounds, which enriched my perspective on life. However, I do miss the comfort of my familiar hometown at times. || Speaker 2: Moving to Japan was [22]a decision motivated by curiosity[/22], to truly immerse myself in a culture I'd long admired. Initially, there were challenges, like the language barrier, but those gave way to a more profound understanding of Japanese customs. [27]What I find most rewarding is how connected I've become to this community[/27]. It's deeply gratifying to be accepted and to feel at home. Of course, I miss my family, but technology bridges that gap. || Speaker 3: For me, moving to Brazil was about [23]finding a work-life balance[/23] that I couldn't achieve in the States. The lifestyle here is less hurried, which intrigued me. Although I faced hurdles, like adjusting to a different climate and pace of life, it was worth it. [28]The most satisfying aspect is embracing a slower, more meaningful way of living[/28]. It's not just about work anymore; it's about truly living and enjoying each day as it comes. || Speaker 4: My move to Kenya was driven by [24]a passion for environmental conservation[/24]. The organization I joined offered opportunities that perfectly aligned with my values. The work we do is significant, and being on the ground gives a sense of purpose. Despite the challenges, like adapting to a simpler lifestyle, [29]it's the impact we make that's most rewarding[/29]. I see the difference every day, and that's truly inspiring. || Speaker 5: The decision to shift to New Zealand came from a need to [25]escape the hustle of urban life[/25]. I've always loved nature, so moving here felt like entering a paradise. Adjusting took time, particularly to the rural setting. However, [30]the tranquility and closeness to nature are unparalleled[/30]. Every morning, I wake up to bird songs instead of traffic noise. It's the peace I've always sought, and it keeps me grounded.

task1.title: Task One
task1.instruction: For questions 21–25, choose from the list (A–H) why each speaker decided to move abroad for work.

questions.speaker: Speaker 1
questions.explanation: Speaker 1 moved for career advancement opportunities.

questions.speaker: Speaker 2
questions.explanation: Speaker 2 was motivated by curiosity about Japanese culture.

questions.speaker: Speaker 3
questions.explanation: Speaker 3 sought a better work-life balance.

questions.speaker: Speaker 4
questions.explanation: Speaker 4 was driven by a passion for environmental conservation.

questions.speaker: Speaker 5
questions.explanation: Speaker 5 wanted to escape the hustle of urban life and be closer to nature.

options.A: A desire to escape urban life
options.B: To find a better work-life balance

task2.title: Task Two
task2.instruction: For questions 26–30, choose from the list (A–H) what each speaker finds most rewarding about their new life abroad.

questions.speaker: Speaker 1
questions.explanation: Speaker 1 finds personal growth the most rewarding aspect of his experience.

questions.speaker: Speaker 2
questions.explanation: Speaker 2 values the connection with the local community.

questions.speaker: Speaker 3
questions.explanation: Speaker 3 appreciates living a slower, more meaningful life.

questions.speaker: Speaker 4
questions.explanation: Speaker 4 finds the impact of their work most rewarding.

questions.speaker: Speaker 5
questions.explanation: Speaker 5 enjoys the tranquility and closeness to nature.

options.G: The tranquility and closeness to nature
